assets.name: Basketball
assets.prompt: orange basketball with dark seam lines, realistic pixel art
assets.notes: 64x64 side view, used in-game and on landing

assets.name: Hoop
assets.prompt: basketball hoop with red rim, white net, square backboard, side view, clean pixel art
assets.notes: 160x160 reference sprite; gameplay draws vector hoop on top

assets.name: Trophy
assets.prompt: trophy gold cup pixel art icon

assets.name: Arena Background
assets.prompt: Dramatic indoor basketball arena at night, wide-angle view from the free throw line toward the hoop end, bright stadium spotlights, polished wooden court floor with reflective shine, blurred crowd in the stands with glowing phone lights, confetti falling, cinematic sports poster art, vibrant orange and deep blue lighting, high detail digital painting

assets.name: Street Court Background
assets.prompt: Stylized street basketball court at sunset, graffiti-covered brick wall backdrop, chain-link fence, cracked asphalt, warm golden light, comic book illustration style, no people, no basketball, no hoop
assets.notes: Alternate backdrop in landing artwork strip

assets.name: HOOPSHOT Logo
assets.prompt: Bold retro sports arcade logo for a basketball shooting game called HOOPSHOT, chunky 3D lettering with orange basketball textures, dripping neon highlights, blue and gold color scheme, comic pop-art style, on pure transparent background

assets.name: Swish
assets.prompt: basketball swish through a net, clean, short

assets.name: Bounce
assets.prompt: basketball bouncing once on hardwood floor, short thump

assets.name: Rim Clang
assets.prompt: metallic clang of a basketball hitting a hoop rim

assets.name: Backboard
assets.prompt: thud of a basketball hitting wooden backboard

assets.name: Crowd Cheer
assets.prompt: short burst of a cheering crowd after a score

assets.name: Buzzer
assets.prompt: loud basketball game buzzer, end of quarter

assets.name: Charge-up Whoosh
assets.prompt: low whoosh charge-up energy sound, short
assets.notes: ElevenLabs, played when pulling back slingshot

reports.summary: Shipped a complete slingshot basketball game vs CPU with 9 progressive rounds, landing page, generated art and sound, and full asset gallery in a single milestone.
changes: Generated basketball, hoop, and trophy sprites via PixelLab map_object API
Generated arena background, street background, and HOOPSHOT logo via OpenAI gpt-image-1
Generated 10 sound effects via ElevenLabs sound-generation API
Canvas game engine: gravity, wind, rim collision with elastic reflection, backboard deflection, floor bounce, trajectory prediction arc
Slingshot input with drag-to-aim, dotted arc preview, power meter line, release-to-shoot (touch + mouse)
9-round alternating turn system with swish=3 / bank=2 scoring
CPU AI solves approximate launch toward hoop and applies per-round jitter that scales with round number
Progressive difficulty: hoop shrinks each round, moves starting round 3, wind kicks in starting round 4
HUD: player score, CPU score, round indicator, turn indicator, wind arrow, score banner, game-over overlay
Particle system: rim spark bursts and confetti on successful shots
Landing page with animated logo (float+pulse), orbiting spinning balls, starfield, IntersectionObserver reveal-on-scroll sections (pitch, features grid, how-to-play, artwork strip, credits)
Asset gallery populated in public/demo-assets/assets.json with all 16 assets
bugs: Ball can linger on rim when bouncing nearly vertically due to 1.6 restitution — intentional but can look floaty
Drags under 12px are silently cancelled with no UI hint
CPU aim doesn't fully lead a moving hoop, so late-round CPU accuracy drops
Resize during mid-shot doesn't reproject ball coordinates
Wind indicator shows arrow only, no numeric magnitude
suggestions: Add haptic vibration on mobile for scores and rim contact
Add a best-of-3 match mode and a daily-seed challenge
Render an in-canvas CPU thinking indicator
Scale ball trail length with shot power
recommendations: Factor physics constants into a tunable config object before adding more shot types
Replace custom pointer handlers with a small state machine if adding power-ups / multi-touch
Prerender backgrounds at smaller resolution — bg_arena.png is ~2.6MB
ideas: Power-ups: fireball shot that breaks the rim for bonus points
Unlockable courts using bg_street.png and other backdrops
Online high-score leaderboard keyed to a daily seed
reports.notes: Everything generated from scratch in one pass: code, art (PixelLab + OpenAI), sound (ElevenLabs). No placeholder assets.
